next_steps: Perform serum and urine protein electrophoresis with immunofixation (SPEP/UPEP) and free light chain assay
Assess for non-cardiac manifestations (e.g., carpal tunnel syndrome, neurologic exam, detailed skin examination)
Consider cardiovascular magnetic resonance imaging (CMR) with T1 mapping and extracellular volume (ECV) quantification
Consider genetic testing for transthyretin (TTR) mutations if PYP is equivocal
Review potential for endomyocardial biopsy if non-invasive workup is nondiagnostic
Perform serum and urine protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and serum free light chain assay
Order alpha-galactosidase A activity level and genetic testing for Fabry disease
Perform laryngoscopy to evaluate the cause of hoarseness
Quantify myocardial extracellular volume using CMR T1 mapping
Obtain NT-proBNP and high-sensitivity troponin levels
Refer for genetic counseling and comprehensive hereditary cardiomyopathy panel
